# Error Handling Checklist: Retro-migration - Version Marker and State-Keyed Backfill/Relocate

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of PRSG-011 error-handling requirements for dry-run/apply separation, clean-tree apply blocks, forced backups, idempotent reruns, partial failure recovery, and backup path reporting.
**Created**: 2026-06-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Dry-Run and Apply Separation

- [x] CHK001 - Is exactly one mode flag required for each migration command so dry-run and apply behavior cannot be mixed or inferred implicitly? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, §FR-010; Contract §Inputs]
- [x] CHK002 - Are all dry-run modes required to be read-only and safe on dirty trees, with no backup creation, file writes, moves, stamps, or generated-zone updates? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Edge Cases; Contracts §Dry-Run Behavior]
- [x] CHK003 - Are all apply/mutation paths required to hard-fail on dirty trees before backup creation or any repository/spec mutation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, §SC-002; Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK004 - Does the output contract require dirty-tree state and an apply-blocked reason, so a dirty apply failure is operator-actionable rather than just an exit code? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, §FR-021; Contract §Dirty-Tree Rules]

## Pre-Mutation Blocks

- [x] CHK005 - Are invalid active-feature states required to block every mutating mode before backup or mutation, while dry-run reports the invalid state without mutating? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009; Data Model §ActiveFeatureState]
- [x] CHK006 - Are collision, frozen/in-flight, and missing or non-regular `SPEC-MOC.md` states specified as pre-mutation blocks for Tier-2 apply? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, §FR-015, §FR-016; Contract §Apply Behavior]

## Forced Backup Semantics

- [x] CHK007 - Does the spec require `migrate-structure.sh --apply` to create a forced, non-skippable backup before repository marker writes or generated-zone updates? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec §FR-012; Plan §Constraints; Contract §migrate-structure Apply Behavior] - `FR-012` now applies to any apply mode with pending mutation, including repository marker writes and generated-zone updates.
- [x] CHK008 - Are backup creation rules stated for every apply path that will mutate, rather than only for Tier-2 relocation? [Resolved, Consistency, Spec §FR-012; Plan §Constraints; Contracts §Apply Behavior] - Backup creation is now framed as a cross-command apply rule for pending mutations.
- [x] CHK009 - Are dry-run reports required to include a planned backup path without creating it, and apply reports required to include the created backup path? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, §FR-012; Contracts §Output]

## Idempotent Reruns

- [x] CHK010 - Does idempotency require completed migration apply reruns to be side-effect-free no-ops, including no backup creation when no mutation is pending? [Resolved, Measurability, Spec §FR-006, §FR-018, §SC-007; Contracts §Idempotency] - Both migration commands now require no-backup/no-mutation no-op reruns when already current.
- [x] CHK011 - Are no-op current-state results represented in operator-facing JSON for both repository migration and Tier-2 relocation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, §FR-018, §FR-021; Contract §Enumerations]

## Partial Failure Recovery

- [x] CHK012 - Does the report contract distinguish post-backup partial failures such as move, stamp, marker-write, or generator failures from pre-mutation blocked states, and require recovery output that names the created backup path when available? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec §FR-021; Contract §status, §items[].action, §Field Semantics] - `FR-021` and the shared report contract now separate `blocked_*` pre-mutation states from `failed_*` post-backup states and require recovery output with the backup path when a backup exists.
- [x] CHK013 - Are target collisions required to fail before mutation, so recovery guidance is not used as a substitute for preventing known unsafe overwrites? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, §FR-015; Contracts §Review Packet Canonicalization, §Evidence Normalization]
- [x] CHK014 - Are recovery instructions required to be operator-facing and tied to a backup path, rather than only implied by the existence of a backup directory? [Clarity, Spec §US2 AS4, Key Entities; Contract §Output]

## Reporting and Operator Safety

- [x] CHK015 - Does the JSON schema distinguish pending, applied, blocked, protected, skipped, backup, recovery, and no-op decisions in a deterministic order? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021; Contract §Enumerations, §Determinism Rules]
- [x] CHK016 - Are scaffold and autopilot flows required to use static suggestion behavior only, with no automatic dry-run or apply execution? [Coverage, Spec §FR-020, §FR-026; Plan §Increment 2]
- [x] CHK017 - Are backup root/timestamp overrides limited to deterministic tests so production reports do not depend on random values or host-specific fields? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions; Contract §Determinism Rules]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK018 - Do success criteria measure dirty-tree apply blocks, dry-run no-mutation, Tier-0 no-stamp behavior, Tier-2 move/protect behavior, and idempotent reruns? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001..SC-007]

## Rerun Verification

- [x] CHK019 - Does `FR-012` now make forced, non-skippable backups a cross-command rule for every apply path with pending mutation? [Spec §FR-012; Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK020 - Do repository and Tier-2 idempotency requirements both prohibit backup creation when an apply rerun has no pending mutation? [Spec §FR-006, §FR-018, §SC-007; Contracts §Idempotency]
- [x] CHK021 - Does the shared JSON report contract now distinguish pre-mutation blocked statuses from post-backup partial-failure statuses and item actions? [Contract §status, §items[].action, §Failure and Recovery Rules]
- [x] CHK022 - Do quickstart and data model recovery instructions require the created backup path and restore hint for failures after backup creation? [Data Model §BackupPlan, §MigrationReport; Quickstart §Recovery]

## Notes

- Initial run found four gap markers across backup semantics, idempotent reruns, and partial failure recovery/reporting. All four were resolved by tightening `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, `quickstart.md`, and the migration CLI/report contracts.
- Rerun verification added CHK019-CHK022 and found no new gaps.
